@@ -703,7 +703,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-vdpi","title":"TASK: System Health & Agent Performance Analytics","description":"Track system health beyond user analytics: Agent response times, error rates, Agent Mail latency, session load times, popular operators usage, system resource utilization. GA4 tracks users; this tracks the system itself for ops monitoring.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:36:38.880232093-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T09:47:16.810334431-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T09:47:16.810334431-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","labels":["brenner-loop","infrastructure"]} {"id":"brenner_bot-vmrq","title":"Code audit: trace flows + fix obvious bugs","description":"Randomly sample core CLI + web flows, trace execution paths, and fix any obvious bugs/regressions found; keep changes small; add/adjust tests as needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:54:45.946881361-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T22:20:00.128748741-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T22:20:00.128748741-05:00","close_reason":"Completed tribunal response-matching fix + tests"} {"id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","title":"EPIC: Testing Infrastructure & Full Coverage (No Mocks)","description":"# Goal Achieve comprehensive test coverage using **real implementations** where possible. # Mocking Philosophy (CLARIFIED) **✅ DO test with real implementations:** - Internal modules (parsers, state management, utilities) - DOM rendering via happy-dom - React hooks and components - Data transformations and business logic **✅ ACCEPTABLE to mock/stub:** - `framer-motion` - Animation timing causes test flakiness - External network calls in **unit tests** (not E2E) - for isolation - Browser APIs not available in test env (IntersectionObserver, etc.) **❌ DO NOT mock:** - Internal module dependencies (import real modules) - Component children (render real children) - State management (use real stores) # Current State (2025-12-30) - **Unit tests**: 41 files, 50% coverage threshold - **E2E tests**: 5 spec files - **Logging**: Excellent infrastructure exists # Critical Gaps **Unit Tests Missing:** - 16+ UI components (SpotlightSearch, DistillationViewer, SessionForm, nav, etc.) - Library modules (corpus.ts, prompt-builder.ts, search/*) **E2E Gaps:** - Search workflow (MAIN USER JOURNEY - untested!) - Session lifecycle, error handling, auth flows # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] 80% line coverage (up from 50%) - [ ] All interactive components tested with real DOM - [ ] All critical user journeys have E2E coverage - [ ] CI fails on coverage regression # Structure - **Unit Tests**: Real DOM, real state, real parsers - **E2E Tests**: Real application, real data, detailed logging - **Infrastructure**: Fixtures, coverage enforcement, accessibility","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:49:24.662121237-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T14:34:47.75397559-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T14:34:47.75397559-05:00","close_reason":"All children completed: Test Infrastructure Enhancements (4ep3), Unit Test Coverage (8dy3), E2E Integration Tests (hvi7). Coverage thresholds enforced, real implementations used, CI integration complete.","labels":["testing quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:35:55.792252655-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-vn19","title":"Add demo mode to session detail page","description":"# Task: Demo Mode for Session Detail Page ## Context Parent feature: Demo Sessions Page (brenner_bot-0y2e) DEPENDS ON: Demo fixture data task (brenner_bot-tdvc) ## CRITICAL PROBLEM Currently, when lab mode is disabled: 1. Sessions LIST page shows LockedState → we're fixing this with DemoSessionsView 2. But session DETAIL page ALSO shows LockedState! From `src/app/sessions/[threadId]/page.tsx` line 319: ```typescript if (!isLabModeEnabled()) { return ; } ``` So when a user clicks a demo session card, they navigate to `/sessions/demo-bio-nanochat-001` and see... a locked page! This breaks the demo experience. ## Solution Overview 1. Detect demo sessions by threadId prefix 2. Load demo messages from fixtures 3. Render session content with demo banner 4. Handle interactive elements appropriately ## Code Changes Required ### 1. Import demo utilities ```typescript import { isDemoSession, getDemoThreadMessages, type DemoMessage } from '@/lib/fixtures/demo-sessions'; ``` ### 2. Handle demo sessions BEFORE lab mode check ```typescript export default async function SessionDetailPage({ params }) { const { threadId } = await params; // Handle local sessions (existing behavior) if (threadId.startsWith(LOCAL_SESSION_PREFIX)) { return ; } // *** NEW: Handle demo sessions *** if (isDemoSession(threadId)) { const demoMessages = getDemoThreadMessages(threadId); if (!demoMessages) { return ; } return ; } // THEN do normal lab mode check for real sessions if (!isLabModeEnabled()) { return ; } // ... rest of existing logic } ``` ### 3. Create DemoSessionDetail component Either refactor existing rendering into a shared component, or create a dedicated demo version that: ```typescript function DemoSessionDetail({ threadId, messages }: { threadId: string; messages: DemoMessage[] }) { // Convert DemoMessage[] to AgentMailMessage[] shape for existing components const agentMailMessages = messages.map(m => ({ ...m, // Add any missing fields with defaults })); // Compute status using existing function const status = computeThreadStatus(agentMailMessages); return (
{/* Demo Banner - PROMINENT */} {/* Header */}

Session

{threadId}
{/* NO RefreshControls in demo mode */} ← Back to Sessions
{/* Round Indicator - use computed status */} {/* Status Section */} {/* NO SessionActions in demo mode - or show disabled state */} {/* Compiled Artifact - if exists */} {status.latestArtifact && } {/* Thread Timeline - render messages */}
); } ``` ### 4. Handle Interactive Elements in Demo Mode #### SessionActions Component The `` component has buttons for: - Compile artifact - Request critique - Post delta - Run experiment **In demo mode, these should NOT be shown** because they call API endpoints that require lab mode. Options: A) **Hide completely** (simplest) B) **Show disabled state with tooltip** explaining demo mode C) **Show placeholder** with explanation of what each action does Recommended: **Option A or C** - either hide or show educational placeholder: ```tsx {isDemoMode ? ( ) : ( )} ``` #### RefreshControls Component The `` component polls for real-time updates. **In demo mode, this is unnecessary** and might cause console errors. **Action**: Do not render RefreshControls in demo mode. #### Sub-page Links The header has links to: - Evidence Pack (`/sessions/{id}/evidence`) - Test Queue (`/sessions/{id}/test-queue`) **In demo mode**: Keep the links - they'll navigate to demo preview pages (handled by brenner_bot-tyyh) ### 5. Demo Banner Component ```tsx function DemoBanner() { return (

Demo Session

This is example data demonstrating a Brenner Loop research session. Actions like Compile and Critique are disabled in demo mode.

← All Demo Sessions Set Up Locally →
); } ``` ### 6. DemoSessionNotFound Component For invalid demo session IDs: ```tsx function DemoSessionNotFound({ threadId }: { threadId: string }) { return (

Demo Session Not Found

The demo session "{threadId}" doesn't exist.

View Available Demo Sessions
); } ``` ## Implementation Approach Options ### Option A: Refactor into shared rendering (Recommended) Extract the main rendering logic into components that work with both real and demo data: ``` SessionDetailPage ├── DemoSessionDetail (isDemoMode=true) │ └── SessionDetailContent (messages, isDemoMode=true) └── RealSessionDetail (isDemoMode=false) └── SessionDetailContent (messages, isDemoMode=false) ``` ### Option B: Separate demo component Create a self-contained `DemoSessionDetail` that duplicates some rendering but is simpler to implement. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Clicking demo session card navigates to detail page (not locked) - [ ] Demo detail page renders thread messages correctly - [ ] Demo banner prominently displayed - [ ] Session status/phase computed and displayed correctly - [ ] All message types render (KICKOFF, DELTA, COMPILED, CRITIQUE) - [ ] SessionActions hidden or disabled in demo mode - [ ] RefreshControls not rendered in demo mode - [ ] Sub-page links present (Evidence Pack, Test Queue) - [ ] 'Back to Sessions' link works - [ ] Invalid demo ID shows friendly error - [ ] No console errors - [ ] Works on Vercel deployment ## Dependencies - Demo fixture data (brenner_bot-tdvc) - need the messages - Coordinates with: Sub-pages task (brenner_bot-tyyh)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T11:04:18.766509438-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T11:32:54.530694783-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T11:32:54.530694783-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vn19","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-tdvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T11:04:29.492802266-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-vn19","title":"Add demo mode to session detail page","description":"# Task: Demo Mode for Session Detail Page ## Context Parent feature: Demo Sessions Page (brenner_bot-0y2e) DEPENDS ON: Demo fixture data task (brenner_bot-tdvc) ## CRITICAL PROBLEM Currently, when lab mode is disabled: 1. Coordinates with: Sub-pages task (brenner_bot-tyyh)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T11:04:18.766509438-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T12:34:22.390405647-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T12:34:22.390405647-05:00","close_reason":"Added demo mode to session detail page with demo banner, disabled actions, message timeline","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vn19","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-tdvc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T11:04:29.492802266-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-voa","title":"TanStack Query: Migrate Document Viewers to Query Hooks","description":"# Goal Migrate TranscriptViewer and DistillationViewer to use the new Query hooks instead of receiving data as props from Server Components. ## Background Currently, document viewers receive pre-fetched data as props: ```typescript // TranscriptViewer.tsx interface TranscriptViewerProps { data: TranscriptData; // Passed from Server Component } ``` This works but means: 1. Data is re-fetched on every navigation 2. No caching between page visits 3. Server must parse on every request ## Migration Strategy ### Option A: Keep Server Fetch + Hydrate (Recommended) Server Component prefetches, passes to client via HydrationBoundary. - Best for SEO (content in initial HTML) - Best for performance (no client-side loading flash) - Query still provides caching for subsequent navigations ### Option B: Full Client-Side Fetch Remove server prefetch, let Query handle everything. - Simpler code - Shows loading state on first visit - Worse SEO **We choose Option A** because the corpus pages are content-heavy and benefit from SSR. ## Implementation ### 1. Update corpus/[doc]/page.tsx ```typescript import { HydrationBoundary, QueryClient, dehydrate } from '@tanstack/react-query'; export default async function CorpusDocPage({ params }) { const queryClient = new QueryClient(); await queryClient.prefetchQuery({ queryKey: ['corpus', 'doc', params.doc], queryFn: () => readCorpusDoc(params.doc), }); return ( ); } ``` ### 2. Update TranscriptViewer ```typescript export function TranscriptViewer({ docId }: { docId: string }) { const { data, isLoading, error } = useCorpusDoc(docId); if (isLoading) return ; if (error) return ; // ... rest of component } ``` ### 3. Update DistillationViewer similarly ## Considerations ### Skeleton Components Create loading skeletons that match the layout: - TranscriptSkeleton: TOC + content placeholders - DistillationSkeleton: Header + section placeholders ### Error Boundaries Create error UI for: - Document not found - Parse failure - Network error ### Backward Compatibility During migration, support both patterns: - Accept data as prop (old pattern) - Use Query if docId provided (new pattern) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] TranscriptViewer uses Query hook - [ ] DistillationViewer uses Query hook - [ ] SSR still works (content in initial HTML) - [ ] Navigation between docs is instant (cached) - [ ] Loading states are graceful - [ ] Error states are handled ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/app/corpus/[doc]/page.tsx - apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptViewer.tsx - apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationViewer.tsx ## Files to Create - apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptSkeleton.tsx - apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationSkeleton.tsx","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:15:54.657062985-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","close_reason":"Migrated document viewers to TanStack Query hooks with HydrationBoundary for SSR prefetching and navigation caching. Created DocumentContentClient.tsx client component that uses useCorpusDoc hook.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-voa","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1r5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:16:17.466921916-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-vtl8","title":"TASK: Agent Quota & Usage Limits Definition","description":"Define resource limits for sustainability. Include: How many agent consultations per session/day/month?, What happens when limit is reached?, Free tier vs paid tier definition, Rate limiting on agent dispatch, Usage tracking in analytics. Agents aren't free - need a plan for costs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:35:54.934413353-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T03:37:56.676237065-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T03:37:56.676237065-05:00","close_reason":"Out of scope - SaaS thinking incorrectly applied. The operator runs their own agents and pays for their own API access. There is no metering/quota system needed.","labels":["brenner-loop","infrastructure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vtl8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:36:14.606519505-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-vtz","title":"Bug: install.sh --dry-run should not edit shell config","description":"In install.sh, configure_path currently appends PATH lines to shell rc when --easy-mode is set even if --dry-run is true. Dry-run should be side-effect free. Also avoid set -u crashes when SHELL is unset; warn + return.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T04:30:33.336980724-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T04:33:37.461190888-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T04:33:37.461190888-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed install.sh configure_path to be side-effect free in --dry-run, and guarded unset SHELL under set -u.","labels":["installer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-vtz","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.2.4","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T04:30:33.341541068-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
